title: Tik (Rogue Squadron)
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image= |name=Tik |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'', which include Tik's [[Death/Legends|death]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], to [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lost two, Lead. The one that almost got you dropped that big black hole on his tail as Eleven was closing. Dinger flew into it and never knew what hit him. Twelve got ripped up by it. Tik is extravehicular, negative life signs.|Alinn Varth|}} '''Tik''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who flew as [[Rogue Twelve]] in [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command's]] [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25.2 ABY]]. The elite twelve-person [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[T-65A3 X-wing starfighter|X-wing fighters]] was led by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gavin Darklighter]], and Tik flew within [[Three Flight]], a [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] group of four commanded by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Alinn Varth]]. When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Borsk Fey'lya]], ignored reports of a mysterious [[Alien/Legends|alien]] threat called the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] that was lurking in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]] recruited Rogue Squadron to clandestinely investigate, under the guise of simulating [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] attacks. After [[Second skirmish in the Outer Rim|capturing]] the [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[Urias Xhaxin]], who had already [[Skirmish in the Outer Rim|tangled]] with the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Yorik-et|coralskipper]] fighters, the Rogues spent their [[Time/Legends|time]] aboard Kre'fey's ''[[Ralroost]]'' running [[Skipchaser|simulations]] against the coralskippers. They later returned to the location of Xhaxin's earlier [[Starfighter combat/Legends|skirmish]] and [[Third skirmish in the Outer Rim|engaged]] a group of the alien fighters, and Tik was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when his [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|fighter]] flew into a miniature [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]] created by a coralskipper's [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulating [[dovin basal]]s. His place in the squadron was filled by a new pilot, [[Anni Capstan]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Snoop, report.''"
"''Good here, Lead. I'm clear. Got it all, including the one that took Eleven and Twelve.|"Snoop" reports to Gavin Darklighter on capturing footage of the deaths of Tik and Dinger|}} Tik was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' confirms that [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] operates within [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]]. in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] as a member of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. Led by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gavin Darklighter]], the Rogues were a twelve-person fighter [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] who [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] [[T-65A3 X-wing starfighter]]s. Tik flew as [[Rogue Twelve]] in [[Three Flight]], a [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] group commanded by [[Rogue Nine/Legends|Rogue Nine]], [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Alinn Varth]]. It also included [[Rogue Eleven/Legends|Rogue Eleven]], [[Dinger]], and [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]]. In 25 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]] recruited Rogue Squadron to simulate [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] attacks in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] so that his forces could pursue them. [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]] was ignoring warnings of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a deadly [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|threat]] lurking in the Outer Rim, but Kre'fey was independently investigating, pretending to prepare for piracy so as not to openly defy the [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] of State. The Rogues were given complete freedom of movement. [[File:RogueSquadron-Aurebesh.svg|thumb|right|200px|The crest of Rogue Squadron]] Rogue Squadron was transported to the Rim aboard the admiral's [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]], the ''[[Ralroost]]''. When they received reports of a [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] encountering unknown fighters in an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]], they ceased their pirate activity and went with Kre'fey to the [[Unidentified star system (Outer Rim)|asteroid belt's system]]. Darklighter led Tik and the rest of the Rogues into the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]], where they were [[Second skirmish in the Outer Rim|attacked]] by a group of salvaged [[Ugly/Legends|ugly]] fighters whose [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], [[Hull/Legends|hulls]], and [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] were severely underpowered. Darklighter convinced the group's leader, the [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[Urias Xhaxin]], to surrender, and aboard the ''Ralroost'', Xhaxin reported that his forces had been [[Skirmish in the Outer Rim|attacked]] by mysterious fighters before blindly jumping to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]] and ending up at their current location. The fighters had used [[Gravity/Legends|gravitic anomalies]] to absorb laser blasts and had fired [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] that ate into their enemies' suddenly unshielded hulls. The information was consistent with data that Kre'fey already had on the Yuuzhan Vong fighters: They possessed [[Biot|living creatures]] called [[dovin basal]]s that created miniature [[Black hole/Legends|black holes]], which could absorb laser blasts and bring down opponents' shields. While a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] was sent to the deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]] location where Xhaxin had been attacked, the Rogues spent their [[Time/Legends|time]] aboard the ''Ralroost'' running [[Skipchaser|simulated]] battles with the Yuuzhan Vong fighters, nicknamed [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] collected data proved inconclusive, so Rogue Squadron escorted a [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter]], designated "[[Snoop]]," back to the location so that data could be collected by a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]. After emerging from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the squadron flew cover for Snoop, with Three Flight positioned off its port bow. When a group of coralskippers [[Third skirmish in the Outer Rim|attacked]], the Rogues tried experimental countermeasures: expanding their [[Acceleration compensator/Legends|inertial compensators]] to protect their shields and firing successive cycles of short, low-powered shots that would hit too quickly to all be caught by the coralskippers' dovin basals. Although the techniques worked, one coralskipper created a large black hole on its tail that destroyed the [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] of both Tik and Dinger. Tik managed to eject from his [[Starship/Legends|craft]] and go extravehicular, but he [[Death/Legends|died]] nonetheless. Footage of both his and Dinger's deaths were captured by Snoop, and the surviving Rogues finished off the coralskippers before recovering Tik's body. His place in the squadron was later taken by a new pilot, [[Anni Capstan]]. ==Personality and traits== Tik was a member of Rogue Squadron, an elite group that served as the New Republic's symbol of starfighter superiority and that included the best pilots in the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]].''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' Colonel Darklighter was proud of Tik and all of the other pilots under his command, feeling that they were a superior fighting team who could have given previous Rogue Squadron rosters a run for their money in simulated [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]]. Tik, however, was killed by the unfamiliar tactics of the Yuuzhan Vong during his first battle with their coralskippers. ==Behind the scenes== Tik briefly appeared in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'', the first novel of [[Michael A. Stackpole]]'s [[2000]] [[Dark Tide duology|''Dark Tide'' Duology]] and the second volume of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] novel series. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' {{1st}} {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Twelve]] |years=–[[25 ABY/Legends|25.2 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Inyri Forge]]''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' |before-years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7.5 ABY]]''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''–[[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] or later |after=[[Anni Capstan]] |after-years=[[25 ABY/Legends|25.2 ABY]]–[[25 ABY/Legends|25.3 ABY]]}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Tik }} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
